Sulfasalazine
5-ASA linked to
sulfapyridine
40–70 mg/kg/day (max
4 g/day)
Digestive disturbances,
headache, rash,
oligospermia, allergic
reactions
CBC, liver and renal tests;
monitor digestive effects
Corticosteroids
(Prednisone)
Systemic
corticosteroid
1 mg/kg/day (max 40 mg/day)
oral (p.o.) for 2–3 weeks,
then gradual taper
Hyperglycemia, weight gain,
hypertension, osteoporosis,
mood disorders, growth
retardation
Clinical monitoring, blood
pressure, weight, blood
glucose, BMD if prolonged
treatment
Budesonide LM
Topical colonic
corticosteroid
According to adult regimen
(left-sided colitis only)
Fewer systemic effects
than prednisone, possible
headache, dyspepsia
Clinical monitoring
Azathioprine
(AZA)
Immunomodulator
(thiopurines)
2–2.5 mg/kg/day
Myelosuppression,
hepatitis, pancreatitis,
nausea, rare risk of
lymphoma
TPMT before treatment,
CBC + ALT/AST every
1–2 weeks then every 3 months,
sun protection
6-Mercaptopurine
(6-MP)
Immunomodulator
(thiopurines)
1–1.5 mg/kg/day
Same as azathioprine
Same as AZA, measurement
of 6-TGN/6-MMP if
ineffective or toxic
